answer.content:
P vs NP Interactive Playground

This single-file interactive demo explores P vs NP concepts with many interactive controls. The UI is intentionally plain: black text on white background, basic controls. Use the controls to build instances, choose solvers, step through algorithms, run experiments, and explore reductions and verification.

Global State

idle 8 100

Problem & Instance Builder

50

Instance Text

Solver Selection & Configuration

Algorithmic Observables

0 0 none

Execution Log & Trace

Experiments & Scaling

The plot shows measured "steps" vs n for the chosen solver and instance generator. For large n, some solvers simulate analytic counts instead of exhaustive enumeration to avoid freezing your browser.

Reductions Explorer

Reduction Output

P vs NP Claim Workshop (interactive)

3

Candidate Algorithm

Misc Tools

messages.content:
Generate a single, self-contained HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript that demonstrates the concept P vs NP with MAXIMUM interactivity and rich user control. Focus on:

- Multiple interactive controls (buttons, sliders, input fields)
- Complex state transitions and user-driven workflows
- Deep exploration capabilities with many possible interaction paths
- Rich functionality as the PRIMARY goal

IMPORTANT: Keep visual design EXTREMELY MINIMAL and BASIC - use simple black text on white background, basic HTML elements with no styling, no colors, no animations. The interface should be functional but visually plain and unpolished. Prioritize interactive richness over visual appeal.

Only respond with the complete HTML file.
